params.name: Torpedo Swimsuit
params.description:
On May 29, 2000, Australian swimmer Ian James Thorpe put on this new swimsuit and got good results.

He won 3 gold medals the year he wore this swimsuit for the first time and shocked the world. This swimsuit imitated shark skin and human tendons at the seams to provide athletes with the power to swim backwards. The material was similar to human skin which greatly reduced water resistance. Due to technical interference, this swimsuit was banned from competitions.

[Swimsuit Evolution]
